plan_metadata.name: Sample Intelligent Data Chart Remote Debug

steps.description: Type text 'intelligent data chart' into the search input field in the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface within Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Create" button next to "Intelligent Data Chart Generator" in the Featured samples list within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
And input ${{var:app_name:intelligent-data-chart-generator}} into the run time variable.

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
env_file="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/intelligent-data-chart-generator/env/.env.local.user"

declare -A values=(
  [SECRET_OPENAI_ENDPOINT]="https://atk-test-open-ai.openai.azure.com"
  [SECRET_OPENAI_API_KEY]="${{secret:AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY:}}"
  [SECRET_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME]="gpt-4.1"
  [SQL_USER]="vscuse-admin"
  [SECRET_SQL_PASSWORD]="${{secret:SECRET_SQL_PASSWORD:}}"
  [SQL_SERVER]="vscuse.database.windows.net"
  [SQL_DATABASE]="intelligent-data-chart"
)

if [[ ! -f "$env_file" ]]; then
  echo "File $env_file not found."
  exit 1
fi

# Backup the original env file
cp "$env_file" "$env_file.bak"

for key in "${!values[@]}"; do
  value="${values[$key]}"
  
  # If the key exists, replace value; else, add to end
  if grep -q "^${key}=" "$env_file"; then
    sed -i "s|^${key}=.*|${key}=\"${value}\"|" "$env_file"
  else
    echo "${key}=\"${value}\"" >> "$env_file"
  fi

done

echo "Updated $env_file with provided values."
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

=== STDOUT ===
Updated /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/intelligent-data-chart-generator/env/.env.local.user with provided values.

✓ Script executed successfully

steps.description:
@code in file: "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/env/.env.dev.user"
find and fill in value: 
SECRET_OPENAI_ENDPOINT="${{env:AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT}}"
SECRET_OPENAI_API_KEY="${{secret:AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY}}"
SECRET_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME="gpt-4.1"
SQL_USER="vscuse-admin"
SECRET_SQL_PASSWORD="${{secret:SECRET_SQL_PASSWORD}}"
SQL_SERVER="vscuse-testing.database.windows.net"
SQL_DATABASE="intelligent-data-chart"

Click on the main editor area of Visual Studio Code, within the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" workspace, at coordinates (600, 267) indicated by a red crosshair, where no active file or UI element is present.
Trigger the "Show All Commands" palette in Visual Studio Code by pressing Ctrl+Shift+P while the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit panel is active.
Type the text 'deploy' into the Visual Studio Code command palette to filter and select the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Deploy" command.
Press the Enter key to select "Microsoft 365 Agents: Deploy" from the VS Code command palette after typing ">deploy".
Click the "Deploy" button in the confirmation dialog to deploy resources in the dev environment within the Visual Studio Code Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface.
@assertion there's "OK to preceed (y)" exist.
Press **Ctrl+Shift+P** to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code, while in the "MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT" workspace.
Type the text 'debug: select and start' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input field at the top center of the interface.
Press the Enter key to select the highlighted "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" command from the command palette in Visual Studio Code.
Type #text:'Launch Remote (Chrome)' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input box at the top center of the interface.
Press the Enter key to confirm selection of "Launch Remote (Chrome)" in the command palette of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Open" button in the confirmation popup for the intelligent-chartdev app, within the Microsoft Teams web interface, to proceed after successful app addition.
Click the "Top 20 selling products" button on the Intelligent Data Chart Generator interface within Microsoft Teams (web version), selecting the first sample query option above the input box.

Click the red "generate chart" button (depicted by a plus icon inside a chart graphic) at the bottom right of the query input box in the Intelligent Data Chart Generator interface within Microsoft Teams.
@assertion there's chart data exist

Click the "Monthly sales data of product 969" button in the Intelligent Data Chart Generator web app interface to select and display sales data for product 969. The button is visually highlighted among other data options, making this interaction targeted and clear.

Click the "Sales statistic by month" button in the Intelligent Data Chart Generator interface within the Microsoft Teams web app. The highlighted position corresponds to the rightmost button in the data query selector, next to "Top 20 selling products" and "Monthly sales data of product 969".
@assertion there's chart data exist

Click the "Sales statistics by month" button in the Intelligent Data Chart Generator web app, accessed via Microsoft Teams in a Chrome browser, to switch the displayed data view. The button is one of three horizontally aligned options above the input box, and the click is visually indicated by a red crosshair overlay.

Click the "Run" button (red-bordered right-arrow) at the lower right of the query input box in the "Intelligent Data Chart Generator" web app within Microsoft Teams, to execute the displayed sales statistics query.
@assertion there's chart data exist
